info.name: CodePulse API Test Suite
info.description:
Production-accurate test suite for the CodePulse Blog Platform API.

JWT is stored in HttpOnly cookies (cookie name: `jwtToken`). Postman's cookie jar handles this automatically when requests are sent with the same domain.

Seeded Admin: admin@myblog.com / 1234
Identity password policy: min 6 chars, no complexity requirements.

item.name: Auth
item.description:
Authentication endpoints: Register, Login (Admin & Reader), Logout, and Get Current User.

JWT is set as an HttpOnly cookie named `jwtToken` with SameSite=None, Secure=true.
Cookie expires in 15 minutes (set by controller), token itself is valid for 60 minutes (set by TokenService).

exec: // Persist the generated email for downstream requests

request.description:
POST /api/Auth/register

Registers a new user with the "Reader" role (default).
Email is dynamically generated via pre-request script for idempotent runs.

DTO: RegisterRequestDto
- Email [Required]
- Password [Required]

Identity password policy: min 6 chars, no digit/uppercase/lowercase/special char requirements.

exec: pm.test('Status code is 400 Bad Request', function () {
pm.test('Response contains identity error about duplicate', function () {
// This test assumes the previous Register request ran first,
pm.test('Response contains email and roles', function () {
// Extract jwtToken from Set-Cookie header for variable storage

request.description:
POST /api/Auth/login

DTO: LoginRequestDto { Email, Password }

Seeded admin credentials: admin@myblog.com / 1234

On success: Sets HttpOnly cookie `jwtToken` and returns { email, roles }.
On failure: 400 with 'Invalid Email or Password'.

exec: pm.test('Response contains email and Reader role', function () {
pm.test('Status code is 400 Bad Request', function () {
pm.test('Response says Invalid Email or Password', function () {
// Ensure admin is logged in first.
// The jwtToken cookie should be present from the Login — As Admin request.
pm.test('Response contains userId, email, role', function () {

header.description: Fallback: manually set cookie if cookie jar isn't populated

request.description:
GET /api/Auth/me
[Authorize]

Returns { userId, email, role } from JWT claims.
Requires valid jwtToken cookie.

POST /api/Auth/logout

Clears the jwtToken HttpOnly cookie by setting it to empty with an expired date.

item.name: Categories
item.description:
CRUD operations for blog categories.

No [Authorize] attributes on this controller — all endpoints are publicly accessible.

DTO fields: Name, UrlHandle (both string, no FluentValidation rules).

exec: pm.test('Response has category with id, name, urlHandle', function () {
pm.test('Each category has id, name, urlHandle', function () {

request.description:
GET /api/Categories

Returns: List<CategoryDto>

GET /api/Categories/{id:guid}

Returns: CategoryDto or 404 NotFound

item.name: Get Category by ID — Not Found (Expect 404)

exec: pm.test('Status code is 404 Not Found', function () {
pm.test('Status code is 204 No Content', function () {
pm.test('Status code is 204 No Content', function () {

request.description:
DELETE /api/Categories/{id:guid}

Returns: 204 NoContent on success, 404 NotFound if not found
Re-creates a category so the Posts folder has a valid category_id to work with.

item.name: Posts
item.description:
Blog post CRUD including soft-delete, restore, hard-delete, stats, and paginated listing.

FluentValidation rules (both Create and Update):
- Title: NotEmpty → 'Title is required.'
- Content: NotEmpty → 'Content is required.'
- UrlHandle: NotEmpty → 'UrlHandle is required.'
- Author: NotEmpty → 'Author is required.'

Most write endpoints require [Authorize(Roles = "Admin")].

exec: pm.test('Response has complete blog post schema', function () {

body.raw:
{
    "title": "Getting Started with Angular 18",
    "content": "Angular 18 introduces a host of powerful features including signal-based components, improved SSR with hydration, and a new control flow syntax. This comprehensive guide walks you through setting up your first Angular 18 project with best practices for modern web development.",
    "publishedDate": "2026-06-12T00:00:00Z",
    "description": "A comprehensive guide to building modern web apps with Angular 18",
    "author": "Navid Munshi",
    "featuredImgUrl": "https://api.blog.munshinavid.me/images/angular-18.jpg",
    "urlHandle": "getting-started-with-angular-18",
    "isVisible": true,
    "categoryIds": ["{{category_id}}"]
}

request.description:
POST /api/BlogPosts
[Authorize(Roles = "Admin")]

DTO: CreateBlogPostRequestDto
- Title (string) — FluentValidation: NotEmpty
- Content (string) — FluentValidation: NotEmpty
- PublishedDate (DateTime)
- Description (string)
- Author (string) — FluentValidation: NotEmpty
- FeaturedImgUrl (string)
- UrlHandle (string) — FluentValidation: NotEmpty
- IsVisible (bool)
- CategoryIds (List<Guid>)

Returns: BlogPostDto

exec: pm.test('Response has paged result schema', function () {

request.description:
GET /api/BlogPosts
[AllowAnonymous]

Query params: query? (string), page (int, default 1), pageSize (int, default 10, max 50)

Returns only IsVisible=true AND IsDeleted=false posts.
Returns: PagedResultDto<BlogPostDto> { Items, TotalCount, CurrentPage, PageSize }

item.name: Get All Blog Posts — With Search Query
Get All Admin Blog Posts (Admin — Includes Drafts & Deleted)

exec: pm.test('Response has paged result schema', function () {

request.description:
GET /api/BlogPosts/admin
[Authorize(Roles = "Admin")]

Returns ALL posts including drafts (IsVisible=false) and soft-deleted (IsDeleted=true).

item.name: Images
item.description:
Image upload and listing.

No [Authorize] on this controller.
Upload uses [FromForm] multipart/form-data.

DTO: ImageUploadRequestDto { File (IFormFile), FileName (string), Title (string) }

exec: // NOTE: This test requires manual file selection in Postman.
// Select a .jpg/.png/.gif file in the 'File' form-data field before sending.
// Status code assertion is skipped because the test will fail without a file.
pm.test('Response time is less than 5000ms', function () {

formdata.description: Select any .jpg/.png/.gif image file from your machine

request.description:
POST /api/Images/upload
[FromForm]

DTO: ImageUploadRequestDto { File (IFormFile), FileName (string), Title (string) }

Validation in controller: File cannot be null and File.Length must be > 0.
Returns: { url: string } — the HTTPS URL to the uploaded image.

exec: pm.test('Status code is 400 Bad Request', function () {
pm.test('Response is an array of images', function () {

request.description:
GET /api/Images

Returns: List<BlogImage> { Id, FileName, Title, FileExtension, FileSizeInBytes, Url }

request.description:
POST /api/Comments
[Authorize] (any authenticated user)

DTO: CreateCommentRequestDto
- Content [Required] (DataAnnotation)
- BlogPostId [Required] (DataAnnotation)

UserId and UserEmail are extracted from JWT claims automatically.
Returns: CommentDto

item.name: Get Comments by Blog Post ID

exec: pm.test('Response is an array of comments', function () {

request.description:
GET /api/Comments/post/{blogPostId:guid}

No auth required. Returns all comments for a given blog post.

exec: pm.test('Status code is 204 No Content', function () {

request.description:
DELETE /api/Comments/{id:guid}
[Authorize]

Owner check: comment.UserId must match current user's ClaimTypes.NameIdentifier.
Admins can delete any comment.
Returns: 204 NoContent, 404 NotFound, or 403 Forbid.

item.name: Authorization & Role Tests
item.description:
Tests verifying that role-based access control works correctly:
- Reader hitting Admin-only endpoints → 403
- No cookie → 401
- Tampered/expired token → 401

body.raw:
{
    "title": "Unauthorized Post",
    "content": "This should fail because Reader cannot create posts.",
    "publishedDate": "2026-06-12T00:00:00Z",
    "description": "Should fail",
    "author": "Reader User",
    "featuredImgUrl": "",
    "urlHandle": "unauthorized-post",
    "isVisible": true,
    "categoryIds": []
}

request.description:
POST /api/BlogPosts requires [Authorize(Roles = "Admin")].
Reader role should get 403 Forbidden.
DELETE /api/BlogPosts/{id} requires [Authorize(Roles = "Admin")].

body.raw:
{
    "title": "Trying to Update",
    "content": "Reader cannot update posts.",
    "publishedDate": "2026-06-12T00:00:00Z",
    "description": "Should fail",
    "author": "Reader",
    "featuredImgUrl": "",
    "urlHandle": "fail-update",
    "isVisible": true,
    "categoryIds": []
}

request.description: GET /api/BlogPosts/admin requires [Authorize(Roles = "Admin")].

GET /api/Auth/me requires [Authorize].
No jwtToken cookie → 401.
Sends a JWT with an invalid signature. The server validates IssuerSigningKey, so this should return 401.

item.name: Unauthorized Comment Delete (Reader deleting another's comment → 403)

exec: // First, login as admin and create a comment
content: 'Admin comment that reader cannot delete',

request.description:
Reader tries to delete a comment owned by Admin.
CommentsController checks: comment.UserId != currentUserId && !User.IsInRole("Admin") → Forbid()

item.name: Negative & Validation Tests
item.description:
Tests derived directly from FluentValidation rules and DataAnnotation [Required] attributes.

FluentValidation (CreateBlogPostRequestValidator & UpdateBlogPostRequestValidator):
- Title: NotEmpty → 'Title is required.'
- Content: NotEmpty → 'Content is required.'
- UrlHandle: NotEmpty → 'UrlHandle is required.'
- Author: NotEmpty → 'Author is required.'

DataAnnotation on RegisterRequestDto: Email [Required], Password [Required]
DataAnnotation on CreateCommentRequestDto: Content [Required], BlogPostId [Required]

Identity password policy: RequiredLength=6, no complexity rules.

exec: pm.test('Admin login for validation tests', function () {
pm.test('Status code is 400 Bad Request', function () {

request.description: FluentValidation rule: RuleFor(x => x.Title).NotEmpty().WithMessage("Title is required.");
